does: This paper introduces Direct Noise Optimization (DNO), a method for aligning diffusion models with continuous reward functions at inference time. Rather than retraining or fine-tuning models, DNO optimizes the noise injected during the sampling process to maximize target rewards like image darkness or aesthetic quality. The approach operates online during generation, making it tuning-free and agnostic to specific prompts. The authors provide theoretical analysis of DNO's properties, develop variants for non-differentiable rewards, and identify a critical failure mode called out-of-distribution reward hacking where optimized samples achieve high rewards but fall outside the pretrained model's support distribution.
connects: This work builds on the growing literature addressing alignment of generative models with downstream objectives, extending beyond earlier supervised fine-tuning approaches that require labeled data or model retraining. It connects to recent inference-time adaptation methods for language and vision models, which similarly aim to modify model behavior without expensive retraining. The paper engages with reward-based alignment frameworks from reinforcement learning applied to diffusion models, building on prior work that uses differentiable reward signals to guide generation. The identified reward hacking problem parallels well-known issues in reinforcement learning where agents exploit reward functions in unintended ways, establishing connections to robustness and distributional safety concerns in learned generative models.
enables: DNO enables practical deployment of reward-aligned generation without model retraining, opening possibilities for end-users to specify custom objectives at generation time. The identification and treatment of out-of-distribution reward hacking suggests future work on distributional constraints and safety guarantees for gradient-based optimization in generative models. This approach could extend to other generative architectures and multimodal reward functions, potentially enabling more sophisticated real-time guidance for applications like controllable image synthesis, design optimization, and personalized content generation while maintaining the quality of pretrained distributions.
